primary_segment: Potash, nitrogen and phosphate crop nutrients plus farm retail
notes: World's largest potash producer (Saskatchewan); one of three players (with Belaruskali, Uralkali) controlling the great majority of globally traded potash.

primary_segment: Phosphate and potash crop nutrients
notes: Large North American phosphate/potash producer (Florida phosphate, Saskatchewan/New Mexico potash) but not a sole-source node; substitutable within the broader market OCP and the potash trio anchor.

primary_segment: Phosphate rock mining and phosphate fertilizer production
notes: State-owned; Morocco holds roughly 70% of world phosphate rock reserves and OCP is the dominant miner/processor of it, the single hardest-to-route-around node in this map.

primary_segment: Nitrogen fertilizers: ammonia, urea, nitrates
notes: One of the two largest Western nitrogen producers; ammonia/urea cost and output are directly hostage to natural gas price and availability, the feedstock chokepoint for nitrogen.

primary_segment: Ammonia and urea nitrogen fertilizers
notes: Largest North American nitrogen producer, advantaged by cheap US shale gas; same gas-linked chokepoint logic as Yara from the other side of the Atlantic.

primary_segment: Potash, phosphate specialty fertilizers and bromine
notes: Significant Dead Sea potash and phosphate producer with a specialty/bromine niche, but not large enough globally to be a sole chokepoint.

primary_segment: Potash and salt
notes: Europe's main potash and de-icing salt producer; meaningful regional supplier but smaller than the Belarus/Russia/Canada trio.

primary_segment: Potash mining
notes: State-owned; with Uralkali, one of the two sanctioned/export-restricted potash giants whose output cannot be casually replaced, a genuine can't-route-around supply node since 2022 sanctions and Lithuanian rail/port cutoffs.

primary_segment: Potash mining
notes: Majority controlled via Uralchem; alongside Belaruskali forms the Belarus+Russia potash bloc that Western sanctions and logistics restrictions have made a persistent supply chokepoint.

primary_segment: Phosphate-based fertilizers and nitrogen products
notes: Large vertically integrated Russian phosphate producer, MOEX Level 1 listed; significant but not irreplaceable given Morocco's reserve dominance.

primary_segment: Diversified nitrogen, phosphate and potash fertilizers
notes: Privately held, HQ redomiciled to Zug in 2015; Russian-origin assets and founder Andrey Melnichenko remain under EU sanctions scrutiny (2025 UK High Court and CJEU rulings addressed ownership/control), but the company itself is not a sole-source node.

primary_segment: Nitrogen fertilizers and low-carbon ammonia/methanol
notes: Dutch nitrogen producer that sold Iowa Fertilizer Company to Koch (closed Aug 2024) and other nitrogen assets to ADNOC/Koch as part of a roughly $7.2B divestment refocusing on low-carbon ammonia and methanol; shrinking footprint, not a chokepoint.

primary_segment: Ammonia and nitrogen fertilizers
notes: Private Russian nitrogen producer and the controlling shareholder behind Uralkali; tracked here mainly for that ownership link.

primary_segment: Potash (Ogden, Utah solar evaporation) and salt production
notes: Small North American potash producer relative to Nutrien/Mosaic; mostly a salt company with a minor potash sideline.

primary_segment: Seeds (Crop Science, ex-Monsanto) and crop protection chemicals
notes: One of the Big Four seed and agrochemical majors (with Corteva, Syngenta, BASF) that between them hold the large majority of global commercial seed genetics and crop protection chemistry; farmers have very few sources outside this group.

primary_segment: Seeds and crop protection
notes: Spun off from DowDuPont in 2019; part of the Big Four seed/agrochem oligopoly.

primary_segment: Seeds and crop protection chemicals
notes: Wholly owned by China's state-owned Sinochem Holdings since the 2017 ChemChina acquisition; withdrew its Shanghai STAR Market listing in March 2024 and as of mid-2026 is reportedly weighing a Hong Kong IPO (~$50B valuation). Part of the Big Four seed/agrochem oligopoly.

primary_segment: Crop protection chemicals (Agricultural Solutions division)
notes: World's largest chemical company by revenue; its Agricultural Solutions division is one of the Big Four crop protection majors.

primary_segment: Crop protection chemicals: insecticides, herbicides, fungicides
notes: Meaningful crop protection specialist (post-2017 DuPont asset acquisition) but smaller than the Big Four and not a sole-source node.

primary_segment: Grain origination and trading, oilseed crushing, food and feed ingredient processing
notes: One of the ABCD grain trading houses (ADM, Bunge, Cargill, [Louis] Dreyfus) that together originate, store, transport and process the large majority of globally traded grain and oilseeds; the group's combined logistics and elevator network is what farmers and importers actually cannot route around.

primary_segment: Grain and oilseed trading and processing
notes: Completed its acquisition of Viterra on July 2, 2025, consolidating two of the world's largest grain handlers; ABCD member.

primary_segment: Grain and oilseed trading, food and agricultural products
notes: Privately held (Cargill-MacMillan family); the largest private company in the US by revenue and the 'C' in ABCD; no public ticker, financials disclosed only voluntarily.

primary_segment: Grain and oilseed merchandising, processing and logistics
notes: Privately held (Louis-Dreyfus family/Margarita Louis-Dreyfus); the 'D' in ABCD; no public ticker.

hq_country: Switzerland (Geneva HQ; parent is China SOE)
primary_segment: Grain, oilseed, sugar and cotton trading
notes: Overseas trading arm of COFCO Corporation, a centrally administered Chinese state-owned enterprise; built via the Noble Agri and Nidera acquisitions into a top-tier trader increasingly described as a fifth 'ABCD+C' player, but not yet as structurally entrenched as the original four.

primary_segment: Palm oil, oilseed processing and edible oils
notes: Asia's largest listed agribusiness group and dominant palm oil processor, but its concentration is regional/crop-specific rather than a global grain chokepoint.

primary_segment: Agri commodity trading and processing: grains, animal feed, rice, cotton, edible oils
notes: Formerly the agri-commodities arm of Olam Group (SGX: VC2); Saudi Arabia's SALIC completed a Tranche 1 purchase in April 2026 giving it an 80.01% controlling stake, with Olam Group retaining 19.99% and a put option for the rest; Olam Group itself continues as the separate ofi (Olam Food Ingredients) parent.
